sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Equinix inks 10MW virtual PPA in Singapore with TEPCO
feed.summary: Equinix has signed a 20-year, 10MW virtual Power Purchase Agreement (vPPA) with Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) in Singapore. This agreement involves Equinix off-taking power from a portfolio of rooftop solar assets, marking TEPCO's first deal of this kind outside of Japan. The vPPA provides Equinix with energy from renewable sources without direct physical delivery, utilizing a price-hedge mechanism.

feed.title: The Palo Alto Networks Story - ft. Nir Zuk & Nikesh Arora
feed.summary: This article details the evolution of Palo Alto Networks, from Nir Zuk's initial frustration with stagnant cybersecurity to the development of the Next-Generation Firewall. It covers the company's growth, strategic shift under Nikesh Arora with aggressive acquisitions, and its transformation into a comprehensive cybersecurity platform in the cloud era. The narrative highlights key decisions, leadership changes, and the philosophy of disrupting oneself to stay ahead in a rapidly changing industry.

feed.title: Intel makes U-turn, cancels plan to sell its networking division: Read chipmaker’s statement
feed.summary: Intel has reversed its decision to sell or spin off its Networking and Edge Group (NEX) division, opting instead to keep it as an internal unit. This U-turn comes after a financial boost from the US government, SoftBank, and Nvidia, leading the company to believe NEX is best positioned for success within Intel. The chipmaker had initially planned to divest non-core assets to improve its finances amidst competition.

feed.title: Johnson Controls announces quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International plc announced a quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share, payable on January 16, 2026, to shareholders of record by December 22, 2025. This dividend reflects a 1.4% yield and 8.11% growth from the previous year, extending the company's unbroken dividend payment record since 1887. The announcement follows strong fourth-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations in earnings and revenue, with UBS maintaining a 'Buy' rating.

feed.title: The Boeing Company (BA): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on The Boeing Company, highlighting its rebound in commercial aircraft deliveries, improved operational efficiency in its 737 MAX and 787 programs, and expansion in its defense and global services divisions. Key catalysts for future growth include accretive divestitures, the Spirit AeroSystems acquisition, and a substantial $636 billion backlog, positioning Boeing for a significant operational and financial turnaround through 2026–2028.

feed.title: Panagora Asset Management Inc. Acquires 26,667 Shares of Evergy Inc. $EVRG
feed.summary: Panagora Asset Management Inc. significantly increased its stake in Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) by 486.8% during the second quarter, acquiring an additional 26,667 shares and bringing its total holdings to 32,145 shares valued at $2.216 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the energy company, which has seen varied analyst ratings but a consensus "Buy" with an average price target of $80.72. Evergy recently announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.695 per share.

feed.title: Raytheon and AWS collaborate on satellite mission control
feed.summary: Raytheon, an RTX business, has partnered with Amazon Web Services to enhance satellite data processing and mission control operations using cloud technology. This collaboration integrates AWS's AI and machine learning services to reduce mission costs and accelerate capability delivery for government and commercial space systems. The agreement aims to improve mission data processing, edge computing, and constellation command and control while maintaining high security.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on United Airlines Holdings (UAL) with a 'Buy' Rating | UAL Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on United Airlines Holdings (UAL) with a 'Buy' rating, setting a price target of $132.00. This new coverage contributes to a positive trend in recent analyst ratings for UAL, with an average target price of $122.06 from 19 analysts. However, GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $68.62, suggesting a potential downside despite the "Outperform" brokerage recommendation.

feed.title: Copper giant Freeport's CEO won't count on mergers for growth
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan CEO Kathleen Quirk stated that while the company is open to merger and acquisition opportunities, it is not relying on them for growth. Instead, Freeport expects to achieve up to 50% growth in its U.S. business over the next five years through internal organic growth initiatives. Quirk made these remarks at the Reuters NEXT conference in New York.

feed.title: Has FICO’s Long Term Surge Pushed Its Valuation Too Far in 2025?
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) has experienced a significant long-term stock surge but appears overvalued by traditional metrics despite recent pullbacks. The article utilizes Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Earnings ratio analyses, suggesting FICO is currently 83.4% overvalued and trading at a much higher PE multiple than its industry and fair value. It encourages investors to use "Narratives" for a personalized valuation approach.

feed.title: EQT (EQT) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Momentum in the Natural Gas Market
feed.summary: EQT (EQT) has seen strong share price performance recently, with improvements in natural gas market sentiment driving its stock up. The company is trading just below analyst targets and at a discount to some intrinsic value estimates, suggesting it might still be undervalued. However, its valuation on earnings-based metrics appears less favorable compared to industry peers, posing questions about the sustainability of its premium.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO): Reassessing Valuation After Recent Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) has seen its share price rise by 3% in the last month and 18% over three months, prompting a reevaluation of its valuation. Despite trading below analyst targets, the company's strong innovation in analytical tools and AI integration positions it for future revenue and margin growth. While a narrative suggests it is 5.4% undervalued, risks such as persistent weakness in China or funding pressures could impact its performance.

feed.title: Align Showcases Digital Advancements at GNYDM 2025
feed.summary: Align Technology showcased its latest innovations in digital dentistry at the Greater New York Dental Meeting (GNYDM) 2025, highlighting advancements across its Align Digital Platform, including the iTero Lumina scanner, Align Oral Health Suite, and Invisalign Smile Architect. The company and its subsidiary exocad also hosted educational sessions and provided hands-on experiences with their technology. These offerings aim to enhance clinical outcomes, patient treatment experiences, and practice growth for dental professionals.

feed.title: Targa Resources to Acquire Rival Pipeline Stakeholder Midstream for $1.25 Billion
feed.summary: Targa Resources Corp. announced its plan to acquire Stakeholder Midstream for $1.25 billion, a move that will bolster its presence in the Permian Basin and enhance its capacity for oil and natural gas transport and storage. This acquisition aligns with the increasing demand for natural gas to power AI and data centers, as well as the rapid development of LNG terminals. Targa plans to finance the deal using cash and a $3.5 billion line of credit, anticipating approximately $200 million in annual revenue from the acquired assets.

feed.title: November market review
feed.summary: In November, a late surge in the stock market, primarily driven by renewed expectations of a Fed interest rate cut, pushed the S&P 500 and Dow Jones into positive territory for the seventh consecutive month, while the Nasdaq was slightly down. Although there was a mid-month bout of volatility, particularly among AI tech giants, the market outlook for long-term investors remains positive despite elevated valuations. Several key events occurred in November, including the end of a US government shutdown and easing trade tensions between the US and China.

feed.title: Citigroup Initiates Coverage on Southwest Airlines (LUV) with Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has initiated coverage on Southwest Airlines (LUV) with a "Neutral" rating and set a price target of $38.00 USD. This marks Citigroup's first rating for the airline, and the market will be observing its impact on investor sentiment. Other analysts have recently maintained or adjusted their ratings and price targets for Southwest Airlines, with the average analyst target suggesting a slight downside, while GuruFocus estimates an upside.

feed.title: Synchrony and Mitsubishi Electric Trane Hvac US Llc Renew Relationship to Offer Customers Flexible Hvac Financing for Greater Comfort
feed.summary: Synchrony and Mitsubishi Electric Trane HVAC US LLC (METUS) have renewed their residential consumer financing partnership, effective October 1, 2025. This multi-year agreement, building on a decade of collaboration, allows METUS customers to access flexible financing options for HVAC systems and services through Synchrony. The partnership aims to enhance the customer purchasing experience and support HVAC contractors by providing tools and new product capabilities, especially as demand for energy-efficient HVAC solutions grows.

feed.title: Micron Technology Soars 178% YTD: Should You Still Buy the Stock?
feed.summary: Micron Technology (MU) has seen its shares soar by 177.9% year-to-date, significantly outperforming its peers and the broader tech sector, driven by strong fundamentals and growth in AI, data centers, and automotive sectors. The company's financial performance shows robust revenue and EPS growth, with analysts projecting continued momentum into fiscal 2026. Despite its impressive run, MU stock trades at a lower forward 12-month P/E multiple compared to the sector average and its competitors, suggesting it remains a compelling buy for long-term investors due to its strategic positioning in high-growth tech trends and attractive valuation.

feed.title: How Strong Is AbbVie's Immunology Franchise After Humira's LOE?
feed.summary: AbbVie's immunology franchise has demonstrated robust growth despite Humira's loss of exclusivity (LOE) in 2023, primarily driven by Skyrizi and Rinvoq. These two drugs have exceeded expectations, with combined sales surging 53% year-over-year to $18.5 billion, and are projected to reach over $31 billion by 2027 due to market share gains and new indications. AbbVie is also expanding its presence in oncology and neuroscience, while facing competition from other key players like Johnson & Johnson and Eli Lilly in the immunology market.

feed.title: Dollar General boosts outlook as value pitch draws shoppers
feed.summary: Dollar General has raised its full-year outlook, reflecting its success in attracting value-conscious shoppers. The company reported earnings that exceeded analyst estimates and saw share gains across various product categories. This positive performance highlights the strong position of low-cost retailers amidst consumers seeking deals.

feed.title: Are Rising Earnings Estimates a Solid Reason to Bet on ANET Stock?
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) has seen increased earnings estimates for 2025 and 2026, driven by strong demand for its data center and cloud networking solutions. Despite robust growth potential and market traction in high-speed switching products, the company faces eroding margins due to rising operating costs and supply chain bottlenecks. Investors are advised to exercise caution, as ANET currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: Johnson Controls announces quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International plc (NYSE: JCI) has declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share of common stock. This dividend is payable on January 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on December 22, 2025. The company has a long history of consecutive dividend payments, dating back to 1887.

feed.title: Synchrony Financial stock hits all-time high at 80.4 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial stock has reached an all-time high of $80.45 USD, reflecting a strong performance with a 20.56% increase over the past year and a 37.93% surge in six months, according to InvestingPro data. While the company maintains an "GREAT" financial health score, InvestingPro analysts suggest the stock might be slightly overvalued. Recent positive developments for Synchrony include exceeding third-quarter earnings estimates, maintaining strategic partnerships, and launching new credit card programs.

feed.title: Stryker Promotes 27-Year Veteran Spencer Stiles to President, COO
feed.summary: Stryker has promoted Spencer Stiles, a 27-year veteran of the company, to President and Chief Operating Officer, effective January 1, 2026. Stiles will be responsible for leading the company’s global businesses, strategy, and mergers and acquisitions. His successor as group president, Orthopaedics, will be Dylan Crotty, who has also been with Stryker for 27 years.

feed.title: RTX's Raytheon announces strategic collaboration with AWS to improve services for space customers
feed.summary: Raytheon, an RTX business, has announced a strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to enhance satellite data processing and mission control operations. This partnership will leverage AWS's cloud-based tools and AI/ML services to help Raytheon's customers reduce mission costs, increase program flexibility, and accelerate the delivery of capabilities for national security objectives in space. The collaboration aims to improve mission data processing, bring mission management to the edge, advance constellation command and control, and incorporate AI/ML services for software development and mission operations.

feed.title: Here's Why You Should Add CONMED Stock to Your Portfolio Now
feed.summary: CONMED Corporation (CNMD) is positioned for growth due to the increasing adoption of its high-margin platforms like AirSeal and BioBrace, along with rising robotic procedure volumes and expanding Ambulatory Surgery Center penetration. Despite tariff headwinds and the launch of Intuitive Surgical's dV5 impacting AirSeal, improvements in supply chains and a strategic portfolio review are expected to drive top- and bottom-line growth. The company has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and has consistently beaten earnings estimates.

feed.title: KEYS: AI-driven Wireline growth, margin expansion, and disciplined M&A fuel Keysight's momentum
feed.summary: Keysight's competitive advantage stems from trust, client integration, and a first-to-market approach. Recent growth is fueled by AI demand in Wireline, strong financial discipline, and strategic M&A, with further margin expansion expected. This information is based on an AI-generated summary of Keysight Technologies Inc.'s Citi Annual Global Healthcare Conference audio transcript from December 4, 2025.

feed.title: Raytheon (NYSE: RTX) and AWS expand cloud collaboration to enhance space mission control
feed.summary: Raytheon, an RTX business, has announced a strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to advance satellite data processing, mission control, and space mission management. This partnership will leverage AWS AI/ML services, Outpost edge capabilities, and serverless technologies to reduce mission costs, increase flexibility, and accelerate operational delivery for government and commercial customers. This collaboration highlights RTX's strategy to integrate Raytheon’s space systems with AWS cloud, AI, and ML tools, building on recent partnerships and contracts across various defense sectors.

feed.title: CarMax Shareholder Alert: ClaimsFiler Reminds Investors
feed.summary: ClaimsFiler is reminding CarMax investors that they have until January 2, 2026, to file lead plaintiff applications in a securities class action lawsuit against CarMax, Inc. (NYSE: KMX). The lawsuit alleges that CarMax and its executives failed to disclose material information during the Class Period, particularly regarding a decrease in sales and net earnings, which led to a significant drop in share price. Investors who purchased CarMax securities between June 20, 2025, and November 5, 2025, are affected.

feed.title: Dollar General lifts outlook on strong Q3; to open 450 stores, remodel 4,250 in 2026
feed.summary: Dollar General reported better-than-expected Q3 earnings and sales, with net income surging 43.8% to $282.7 million and net sales rising 4.6% to $10.65 billion. The company plans to open approximately 450 new stores and remodel 4,250 in fiscal 2026, while also raising its full-year guidance for EPS and sales growth. Dollar General ended the quarter with 20,901 stores, demonstrating strong operational performance and market share gains.

feed.title: Proofpoint Named a Leader in 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Email Security
feed.summary: Proofpoint has been named a Leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Email Security for the second consecutive year, recognized for its Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company's comprehensive, human-centric protection addresses evolving email threats like fraud and account takeover, integrating seamlessly with platforms like Microsoft and Google and achieving a 4.6 out of 5 rating on Gartner Peer Insights. Proofpoint continues to innovate with technical integrations that unify email security with broader workspace protection, including API-driven deployment, comprehensive messaging and collaboration protection, and AI-powered threat summarization.

feed.title: FDA OKs Algorithm Updates to Insulet’s Omnipod 5 Insulin Pump
feed.summary: Insulet has received FDA 510(k) clearance for enhancements to its Omnipod 5 automated insulin delivery (AID) system. These updates to the Omnipod 5 algorithm include a lower 100 mg/dL Target Glucose option and a more seamless automated experience. The new features are expected to launch in the U.S. in the first half of 2026 for individuals aged 2 and older with type 1 diabetes and aged 18 and older with type 2 diabetes.

feed.title: GSA Strikes OneGov Deal With Palo Alto Networks
feed.summary: The General Services Administration (GSA) has announced a OneGov agreement with Palo Alto Networks, offering federal agencies discounts of up to 60% on cybersecurity solutions. This deal, aimed at protecting federal cyber infrastructure and accelerating AI adoption, includes discounts on AI security, cloud security, and zero trust solutions. It is part of GSA's broader OneGov initiative to streamline federal IT acquisitions and modernize cybersecurity defenses against AI-powered threats.

feed.title: CHD Analyst Rating Update: Argus Research Lowers Price Target
feed.summary: Argus Research has maintained a "Buy" rating for Church & Dwight Co (CHD) but lowered its price target from $110.00 to $102.00, reflecting revised market expectations. This adjustment comes amidst several other analysts also revising their ratings and price targets for CHD. The company, known for its Arm & Hammer brand and other consumer products, has an average target price of $97.76 among 20 analysts, suggesting a 15.95% upside from its current price of $84.31.

feed.title: Tko Group Holdings, Inc. Declares Fourth Quarter Cash Dividend, Payable on December 30, 2025
feed.summary: TKO Group Holdings, Inc. announced a Fourth Quarter cash dividend, where Class A common stockholders will receive a pro rata share of an aggregate distribution of approximately $150 million. The per share dividend will be $0.78, payable on December 30, 2025, to stockholders of record as of December 15, 2025.

feed.title: Packaging Corp to shutter Washington facility, cutting 200 jobs and incurring $205 million charge
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America announced it will close its Wallula, Washington facility, leading to 200 job cuts and a $205 million charge. The company expects reconfiguration actions to be completed by Q1 2026, aiming to reduce production costs and replace lost capacity through enhancements at other mills. This decision comes after a challenging third quarter marked by subdued demand and higher operating costs.

feed.title: Do Rising Earnings Estimates Strengthen the Case for Qualcomm Stock?
feed.summary: Qualcomm's earnings estimates for fiscal 2026 and 2027 have increased, indicating positive sentiment for the stock, driven by strong Snapdragon traction and growth in its automotive business. However, the company faces challenges from the U.S.-China trade spat, margin pressures, and stiff competition from rivals, making its long-term viability in certain markets uncertain despite portfolio strengths.

feed.title: New York State Common Retirement Fund Sells 80,297 Shares of lululemon athletica inc. $LULU
feed.summary: The New York State Common Retirement Fund significantly reduced its stake in lululemon athletica inc. by selling 80,297 shares, representing a 53.9% decrease in its position. This transaction leaves the fund with 68,607 shares valued at $16.3 million, or 0.06% of the company. Despite beating EPS estimates, lululemon recorded a slight revenue miss and currently holds an average "Hold" rating from analysts with a consensus target price of $227.68.

feed.title: Director Of Las Vegas Sands Makes $2.09M Sale
feed.summary: Irwin Chafetz, a Director at Las Vegas Sands (NYSE: LVS), recently sold 30,000 shares of the company, totaling $2.09 million, as disclosed in an SEC filing on December 4. This insider transaction follows a period of positive revenue growth for Las Vegas Sands, which is a major operator of integrated resorts in Asia. The company's financials indicate strong cost management but also challenges with debt levels and EPS compared to industry averages.

feed.title: HPE Announces Sale of Its Remaining H3C Stake for $643 Million
feed.summary: Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Company (NYSE: HPE) has announced the sale of its remaining 9% stake in H3C for $643 million, bringing the total divestiture to $1.3 billion. Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani reiterated an Outperform rating on HPE, viewing the sale as strategically positive and aligned with HPE's financial goals and Juniper integration. The transaction is expected to close within 180 days, pending regulatory and Unisplendour shareholder approval.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for EPAM Systems
feed.summary: EPAM Systems Inc. (NYSE: EPAM) currently has a P/E ratio of 30.53, which is lower than the IT Services industry average of 36.55. While a lower P/E ratio could suggest undervaluation, it might also indicate expectations of weaker future performance or financial instability compared to its peers. Investors are advised to consider the P/E ratio alongside other financial metrics, industry trends, and qualitative factors for a comprehensive analysis of the company's financial health.

feed.title: Introducing Our FY25 Cisco Foundation Impact Report
feed.summary: The Cisco Foundation has released its FY25 Impact Report, highlighting its work in supporting global communities and organizations for nearly 30 years. The report showcases how the Foundation positively impacted over 154 million lives across 100+ countries, catalyzed over US$42 million in additional funding, and partnered with innovative groups like Trek Medics, FINCA Ventures, Raspberry Pi Foundation, and Digital Green. Moving forward, the Foundation plans to continue supporting established organizations and "hidden gems" in their use of AI and digital innovation for expanded impact.

feed.title: American Water (NYSE: AWK) outlines steps to prevent frozen residential pipes this winter
feed.summary: American Water (NYSE: AWK) has advised customers to prepare their homes for freezing temperatures to prevent costly damage from frozen or burst pipes. The company provided step-by-step guidance including locating and testing the main shut-off valve, insulating exposed pipes, and actions to take when temperatures fall below freezing. American Water also offered tips for safely thawing pipes and recommendations for when customers are away from home.

feed.title: Insulet Announces FDA 510(k) Clearance of Omnipod® 5 Algorithm Enhancements that Redefine Insulin Delivery and Simplify the Pod Experience
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation has received FDA 510(k) clearance for significant enhancements to its Omnipod 5 Automated Insulin Delivery System. These updates include a new 100 mg/dL Target Glucose setting for more customization and improved glucose management, alongside an enhanced algorithm to keep users in Automated Mode longer. The improvements are expected to launch in the U.S. in the first half of 2026 for individuals aged 2 and older with type 1 diabetes and aged 18 and older with type 2 diabetes.

feed.title: Dollar General: A Clean Beat And A Re-Rating In Sight (NYSE:DG)
feed.summary: Dollar General delivered a strong earnings report, outperforming expectations on sales, EPS, and margins, indicating its resilience in an inflationary environment where lower-income consumers are tightening their spending. With improved profitability, reduced inventories, and raised guidance, the company appears poised for a potential re-rating as its valuation remains below competitors like Dollar Tree. This performance highlights the potential for discount chains to thrive amid consumer inflation fatigue.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Freeport-McMoran, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors in Freeport-McMoran, Inc. who purchased securities between February 15, 2022, and September 24, 2025. This action follows significant stock price drops after the company suspended mining operations at its Grasberg Block Cave due to an incident that resulted in fatalities and missing workers. Investors have until January 12, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences (EW) Revises FY25 Revenue Projection Upward
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences (EW) has updated its fiscal year 2025 financial outlook, projecting a revenue increase of 9% to 10% and showcasing strong financial health with robust margins. The company, a leader in medical devices specializing in structural heart disease treatments, provided this revised guidance at its annual investor conference. Despite positive analyst sentiment and strong financial indicators, a P/E ratio close to its 1-year high and recent insider selling activity suggest a mixed valuation picture.

feed.title: Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) Raised to Buy at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial upgraded Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) from a "hold" to a "buy" rating, setting a $23 price target, despite the company's recent significant quarterly EPS miss, high debt-to-equity ratio, and negative net margin. Six Flags has faced mixed analyst views, with a consensus "Hold" rating, but various institutional investors have adjusted their stakes in the company. The theme park operator reported revenues of $1.32 billion for the quarter, slightly below analyst estimates.

feed.title: Pentair (PNR) Sees Analyst Downgrade and Price Target Reduction by Barclays | PNR Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays has downgraded Pentair (PNR) to "Equal-Weight" from "Overweight" and reduced its price target to $115.00 from $127.00. This 9.45% decrease in the price target comes amidst other analysts maintaining or raising their targets for the water treatment industry leader. The average analyst target price for PNR is currently $122.25, indicating a potential upside of 14.82% from its current price, while GuruFocus' GF Value suggests a downside.

feed.title: Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) Rating Lowered to "Hold" at Zacks Research
feed.summary: Zacks Research has downgraded Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) from a "strong-buy" to a "hold" rating, despite the company beating its quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. While the consensus market rating remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $2,118.50, concerns include a high P/E ratio and significant insider stock sales totaling $23.2 million. The company's fundamentals are strong, with EPS of $7.74 and revenue of $515.8 million, up 13.6% year-over-year.
